_comment: 2 entries only — the MA Senate produces very few contested housing roll calls due to its heavily Democratic composition; all other housing votes in the 193rd and 194th sessions passed unanimously or near-unanimously. H4977 final passage (rc 1463317, 37-2) was excluded as too lopsided to differentiate senators. Both entries are CHAPA-confirmed anti-MBTA Communities amendments forced to a roll call by the Republican minority.

bills.description: Anti-MBTA Communities amendment (adds unnecessary appeal criteria to MBTA Communities Act) — CHAPA-confirmed bad amendment
bills.rationale: Senate Amendment 25, Roll Call #194, during S2834 (Affordable Homes Act) debate. 6Y-34N. The 6 YEA votes are anti-housing; NAY = pro-housing.

bills.description: Anti-MBTA Communities amendment (adds unnecessary appeal criteria to MBTA Communities Act) — CHAPA-confirmed bad amendment, FY2026 budget debate
bills.rationale: Senate Amendment #13, during S3 FY2026 budget debate. 5Y-34N. The 5 YEA votes are anti-housing; NAY = pro-housing.
